thechrisdo.text:
Want to come to our Santa Monica studio for a live podcast recording with @AprilynneAlter ?

Mon
12pm

Leveling up your YouTube content.
Grab a ticket here. 

https://t.co/hiiHrMgmDW https://t.co/l731yj5usF

There’s a reason why @RiversidedotFM is the de facto standard when it comes to podcast recording platforms, and why I won’t use anything else—it’s the best.

I’ve trusted it to capture hundreds of conversations with notable guests, authors and thought leaders. I’m not going to take that risk with some other platform.

• No blotchy videos with compression artifacts
• No glitchy internet hiccups to deal with
• Separate, clean audio channels
• Easy to use
• Super fast syncing
• Text based editing tools makes creating short form content a breeze

Comment “Riverside” and I’ll send you a checklist to use to go from recording, to published content in one place.

#riversidepartner

Live in-person recording:
Saturday— Feb 7, 2pm
w/ guest Manager Jon (@jon_cater )

FREE (just DM me)

@thefuturishere  
Santa Monica Office
1702 Olympic Blvd.

Street parking only https://t.co/CkP5iL4ZDN

We live in the age… where my 2 adult sons have "dropped" out of college, launched their own language app by vibe coding—all self-taught. I watch them work late into the night, doing things that I barely understand.  

It's a good time to be alive.
https://t.co/3ki4ZpodkB
“Grief is the price you pay to experience true love.” —@jimmycarr

In the age of AI, the ability to do something slowly will be the new luxury. 

Craft will matter more. 
People will pay more knowing that an artisan made a beautiful object vs a 3d printed version of the exact same thing.
People who don’t have the wisdom to see the real value in people who have life experience (read older) don’t deserve their gifts. Their loss in my opinion.

I'll be in Vancouver doing a fireside chat on "The Future of Brand" in the age of AI.

Sat July 26
6-9pm

Hope to see you there.
https://t.co/TqLBD0qWAD

Come say "hi" if you're attending @VidCon this year.

Unbland Yourself. 

See ya there. https://t.co/vxvd142tYp

In sales, it's essential to create "necessary tension"—
by pausing after you say the price, by asking 'is there room in the budget?', by not immediately agreeing to concessions, by asking 'is price the determining factor?' or 'would you prefer to work with me or someone else?', or by saying 'I can't do that, but let's get creative.'

What are ways you create necessary tension?

I don't think of myself as spiritual.
This definition of spirituality
got me thinking:

"Know, accept and express your truest self."
— @kienvuumd

Everything is an investment—
Time, energy, love, money, attention. 

Successful and happy people recognize poor and good uses of those precious resources.

Your response to “problems” you encounter will determine how far you go, the joy you experience and the things you achieve in life.

Problems are an invitation to come up with a creative solution.

Real relationships aren't built in DMs asking for work, or following some cookie cutter sales template. Is anyone really fooled?

It takes time.
Show up consistently.
Build trust.
Give without expectations. 

Slow down your relationship building
to speed up the close.

Don't design a life where you have machines do the thinking, and you do the labor.
You should do the thinking.
Have the machines do the boring, repetitive, time consuming, labor intensive work.

Deliver the best steak the customer paid for, 
not the best steak you can make,
or you'll be making a business mis stake.

We all need one person to see in us what we can't see in ourselves. To believe that we are capable. To know that we will succeed. To look past our faults and show us grace.

Until one day…
we believe it ourselves.
